summary: The current HEAD preserves the surface-aware freshness runtime contract and closes the design-lineage gap: Story, Architecture, and Spec all declare parent_design vibepro-content-scoped-evidence-freshness, which resolves to the registered design root. Regression, legacy/fail-closed, strict override, generated artifact, config, documentation, and E2E surfaces are covered.
inspection_summary: Read the current architecture review request and parallel dispatch, compared HEAD 6ed774dc77d187c42b894e2f073a50580c72e631 with the prior independently reviewed tree, inspected the design registry and all Story/Architecture/Spec parent_design declarations, verified the formal Spec architecture/code/test anchors, re-read the runtime freshness and content-binding boundaries, reviewed the guides/releases and focused/E2E tests, checked current verification evidence, and reran the eight-case E2E matrix.
inspection_evidence: design-ssot-reconciliation.summary.json reports status=passed, coverage_status=passed, action_items_count=0, unregistered_changed_docs_count=0; current unit evidence is 39/39 at HEAD 6ed774dc; current E2E rerun is 8/8 at HEAD 6ed774dc; git diff --check origin/main...HEAD passed.
judgment_delta: Initial concern: the new parent_design fields might name an unregistered or inconsistent authority across Story, Architecture, and Spec. Final conclusion: all three name vibepro-content-scoped-evidence-freshness, the registry links all three documents under that root, and current reconciliation passes with zero action items or unregistered changed documents.

Initial concern: exact-HEAD lineage repair or review-budget amendment might alter the runtime freshness contract. Final conclusion: changes since the prior reviewed tree are metadata/frontmatter and Story-local bounded review policy only; src/agent-review.js, src/content-binding.js, focused tests, and E2E code are unchanged.

Initial concern under regression_guard: removing the built-in strict roles could weaken legacy, dirty fingerprint, provenance/lifecycle, or explicit strict paths. Final conclusion: the implementation retains fail-closed content evaluation and legacy merge-delta handling, current focused evidence passes 39/39, and the current E2E matrix passes 8/8.

Initial concern under path_surface_coverage: design lineage or operator-facing outputs might disagree with runtime behavior. Final conclusion: formal Spec anchors resolve, design reconciliation passes, English/Japanese guides and releases describe the same content-surface default and reasoned strict override, and generated review policy exposes content_surface rather than a hidden role-name exception.
